failure_modes.text: Bias blind spot amplification lets the team audit everyone else's evidence standards while sparing its own.
failure_modes.mitigation: Turn the diagnostic lens inward first: audit your own evidence standards with the same rigor you apply to others.

failure_modes.text: Premature simplification can harden an early story before contradictory evidence has been tested.
failure_modes.mitigation: Delay story-hardening until contradictory evidence has been actively sought and tested; premature simplification is the gateway to confirmation lock-in.

failure_modes.text: Flawed hypothesis testing turns the search into confirmation work instead of genuine falsification.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require genuine falsification: define what evidence would disprove the hypothesis and look for it before declaring the hypothesis confirmed.

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe for this conclusion to be true, and which of those assumptions is weakest?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: What disconfirming evidence would change our mind if it were true?
Which failed cases, churn cases, or null results are we currently excluding from the packet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Search for information that would prove you wrong, not just information that confirms you are right.

heuristics.text: Use the Darwinian approach by trying to kill weak ideas before defending favored ones.
Ask what you would have to believe so implicit assumptions become inspectable instead of sacred.
heuristics.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Adopt the scientific mindset by treating the claim as a hypothesis that must survive disconfirmation pressure.

curation_notes.summary: This file stays close to the raw failure table and named mitigation heuristics because the source already contains chunk-ready intervention language.
donor_drops: Ignored donor case examples that repeated the same evidence-filtering structure in more runtime-shaped language.
open_questions: The premortem questions are operational normalizations of raw challenge prompts rather than a single explicit pre-mortem block.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on scientific method, dialectic pressure, and outside-view discipline for later relation work.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section provides strong future material around motivated reasoning, social pressure, and identity defense.
structured_tensions: Future tension preservation could be valuable around confirmation-bias versus models that demand disconfirming evidence or outside-view anchoring.
